title: Deploy pss-assembly-viewer canary to Pi and smoke test
description: Deploy the extracted assembly-viewer alongside the live monorepo version using docker-compose.canary.yml. Canary on host port 3107, off platform_net. Live route /assembly/ via gateway must be untouched. Smoke test: page loads at http://10.0.0.75:3107/assembly/, login works, 3D STL viewer renders, API routes (/api/assembly-data, /api/drawing, /api/export-pdf, /api/stl/*) respond, sidebar renders.
acceptance_criteria: Canary container running, healthcheck green, /assembly/ routes render at :3107, parity with live verified, no impact to live :3000/assembly/.

title: Land standalone-mode + extraction docs in platform-portal
description: Commit and push the in-monorepo standalone target, build script branching, and the extraction-guide / skill-doc updates produced during the assembly-viewer extraction. Files: docs/APP_EXTRACTION_GUIDE.md, docs/SKILL_pss_standalone_app.md, docker/node/Dockerfile.prod, scripts/deploy.sh, scripts/deploy-timed.sh, apps/assembly-viewer/next.config.ts, scripts/build-times.csv.

title: Laser header: dim stale state values when device is offline
description: Header shows execution/program/mode/material as if live even when device_status.status='offline'. Should dim/mark these as last-known when offline so operators don't trust stale state.

title: Rebuild Laser dashboard against pss_laser_* schema
description: The Mazak/Laser dashboard at /laserquote/mazak was built against the old mazak_* tables. The confirmed schema uses pss_laser_program_runs, pss_laser_state_events, pss_laser_telemetry with revised columns and semantics. Rebuild as a parameterised route /laserquote/machines/[deviceId] supporting MAZAK-SN328525 today and additional lasers later. Add comment parsing (MATERIAL/dims regex), gas badge from material suffix, NCmode in header, false-start filter (default on), revised utilisation logic (cutting time sums READY+FEED_HOLD ended_states, programs counts distinct programs with >=30s rows, parts = max-min part_count_end over range).
close_reason: Page moved to /laserquote/machines/[deviceId], rebuilt against pss_laser_* schema with comment parsing, gas badge, NCmode, false-start filter, revised utilisation maths, 30-day chart on READY+FEED_HOLD only

title: Add scripts/new-app.sh bootstrap helper
description: CLI script: prompts for a folder name (pss-<appname>), creates C:\Dev\PSS\<name>, copies docs/NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md, writes .claude/CLAUDE.md so Claude auto-reads it on session start, runs bd init in the new folder, opens the folder in a new VS Code window with the doc pre-opened, then opens a Claude Code chat with a pre-filled prompt to scaffold the app.

title: Author NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md starter doc
description: Self-contained scaffolding doc the user can copy into a new pss-<appname> repo. Covers port reservation, file templates (package.json, next.config.ts, Dockerfile, .dockerignore, docker-compose.app.yml, build.sh), gateway wiring, Pi deploy, and an AI-assistant-instructions section. Includes reminders to bd init in the new repo and to point the assistant at beads tooling.

title: Remove apps/assembly-viewer from platform-portal monorepo
description: After canary validation passes: delete apps/assembly-viewer/ from the monorepo, remove the service block from docker-compose.yml / deploy.yml / prod.yml, remove from the loop in scripts/deploy.sh, update docs/PORTS.md row to point at the standalone repo. Promote the canary to be the live stack (rename container, join platform_net, drop the host port mapping so it goes through gateway). Verify gateway /assembly/ still routes correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Monorepo no longer contains apps/assembly-viewer/. Live /assembly/ via gateway resolves to the standalone container on platform_net. No regressions in other apps.

title: 24h soak: monitor canary stability and parity vs live
description: Watch the canary for 24h after deploy. Confirm: no container restarts, healthcheck stays green, no errors in docker logs, no user complaints. Compare behaviour against live. If anything diverges, file a bug and gate the monorepo removal.

title: Timeline: render 06:00–17:00 working-hours window
description: Execution timeline currently spans full 24h. Show working-day window (06:00–17:00 London) instead, with constants so the window can be changed later.

title: Roll out Next.js standalone output to remaining 8 monorepo apps
description: portal, jobcards, documents, timesheets, operations, scanner, laserquote, nesting all still use the legacy runner target. Each opt-in: add output: 'standalone' + outputFileTracingRoot to next.config.ts, add to STANDALONE_APPS list in scripts/deploy.sh and deploy-timed.sh. Expected savings ~45s per app on full rebuild and ~6 GB Pi disk usage. Pace one or two at a time so a regression is easy to attribute.

title: Decide on orphan/untracked files in platform-portal working tree
description: Items flagged but not actioned: (1) apps/assembly-viewer/C:DevPSSplatform-portal — looks like an accidentally-created directory with a literal Windows-path name; (2) apps/assembly-viewer/sample-data.json (4.5 MB) — unclear if test fixture or accidental dump; (3) tools/qrcodes/ — H&S/crane PNGs, generated artifacts vs canonical assets; (4) .vscode/ — IDE settings, depends on team policy. Each needs a yes/no on commit-or-gitignore.

value: Extraction rationale (2026-04-20 retro): the original thesis 'extraction cuts build time' was wrong — per-app build cost is dominated by next build and docker push regardless of repo location. Extraction is for LIFECYCLE ISOLATION (independent start/stop/rollback, separate release cadence) not speed. matl-cert was extracted for compliance reasons; pss-assembly-viewer for canary/cutover practice. Don't extract apps that share heavily with portal/jobcards.

Standalone repo trap (2026-04-20): app/.dockerignore is MANDATORY. Without it, host npm install creates Windows-path symlinks in node_modules for file: deps (@platform/ui/auth/supabase). 'COPY . .' in the builder stage overlays those broken symlinks on top of the container's clean node_modules and the build fails with 'Module not found: Can't resolve @platform/ui'. matl-cert avoided this by chance (no local node_modules existed when first built). Always include .dockerignore that excludes node_modules, .next, .env*, .git.

Centralised .env convention (2026-04-20): platform-portal/.env is canonical for all projects. Siblings reference it via env_file: ../platform-portal/.env in their docker-compose.app.yml. On the Pi, layout is /opt/platform-portal/.env and /opt/pss-<app>/ as siblings. App-specific overrides (PORT, container-name) stay inline in the compose; cross-app secrets/URLs live in the shared file only. Edit secrets in one place.

Standalone-mode rollout (2026-04-20): BuildKit cache patch eliminated the pnpm install tax (~42s/app saved on incremental). Next.js output: 'standalone' + outputFileTracingRoot dropped runtime image from ~800 MB to ~100 MB and push time from ~38s to ~7s. Total full-sweep time barely moved (~1200s) because next build + push still dominate per-app — that's the floor. The real win is on partial rebuilds (~5s for unchanged apps) and on Pi disk pressure.
